summary: Reviewed DinoBrain memories/data and authored docs/OS_COMPLETION_CONDITIONS.md as the completion-conditions document for the user's desired DinoBrain OS direction. Coordinated 16 independent reviewer agents, incorporated the data architecture/performance objection about bounded/index-backed retrieval and cold operational data partitioning, reran final consensus, and reached 16/16 final agreement on the latest document.
decisions: Completion is defined as an evidence-backed OS loop, not a note vault or demo graph.
Current DinoBrain is documented as a strong foundation, not complete under the accepted conditions.

Completion requires live pre-response OS enforcement, fail-closed gates, closed task lifecycle, honest hybrid retrieval, bounded/index-backed candidate generation, graph health, lifecycle cleanup, source provenance, behavior eval, memory-use audit, feedback writeback, Observatory evidence, privacy/public-data safety, new-PC recovery equivalence, and release/sync hygiene.
A disagreement from reviewer 10 was incorporated by adding explicit bounded/index-backed sparse/dense candidate generation and cold operational data partition/compaction/archive requirements.
next_steps: Use docs/OS_COMPLETION_CONDITIONS.md as the governing acceptance document before implementing the next DinoBrain OS changes.
Do not claim DinoBrain OS completion until the mandatory gates and proof registry are satisfied against live current data.
